title: A conditionally executed single line should be denoted by indentation
description: A conditionally executed single line should be denoted by indentationAnalyze your codeconsistency-formattedmaintainabilityCode SmellWhy is this an issue?When the line immediately after a conditional has neither curly braces nor indentation, the intent of the code is unclear and perhaps not what is executed. Additionally, such code is confusing to maintainers.if (condition) // Noncompliant doTheThing(); doTheOtherThing(); // Was the intent to call this function unconditionally?It becomes even more confusing and bug-prone if lines get commented out.if (condition) // Noncompliant // doTheThing(); doTheOtherThing(); // Was the intent to call this function conditionally?Indentation alone or together with curly braces makes the intent clear
recommendation: Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.

checks.message: Use proper indentation or braces for conditionally executed code to improve readability and prevent maintainability issues.
